heading: Walleroobie vs Wallis Lake — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Walleroobie, New South Wales and Wallis Lake, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Walleroobie has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Wallis Lake (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Walleroobie or Wallis Lake safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Walleroobie has a flood risk score of 42/100, while Wallis Lake scores 23/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Walleroobie scores 42/100 for bushfire risk. Wallis Lake scores 43/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Walleroobie or Wallis Lake?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Walleroobie has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
